name: Chicken Provincial
recipeIngredient: Hot rice, mashed potatoes or Noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Disjoint the chicken and cook in a pressure cooker for 15-20 minutes, along with parsley, bayleaf, onion, salt, peppercorns, and celery. If you don't have a pressure cooker, use a large pot and cook over medium heat until the chicken is very tender. Remove the chicken, drain it, and slice off the meat, removing all skin.
2. Do not discard the stock. Keep the chicken warm and skim fat from the stock, reserving 1 cup.
3. In a heavy skillet, heat oil. Stir in flour and cook until brown.
4. Add 1 cup of chicken stock and peas to the skillet. Cover and simmer for 6-8 minutes.
5. Uncover, add milk, lemon juice, and rind, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil and reduce heat, then cook until thickened.
6. On a large platter, place mashed potatoes, rice, or noodles. Arrange the warmed chicken on top and cover with the peas mixture. Garnish with parsley. This dish works well with leftover chicken or turkey.
